zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Cressett於1355年出生在英國史洛普爾厄普頓 可雷瑟特時，他的父親Thomas Cressett26歲，母親Constance de Upton26歲。他於1385年與Florence Jane Corbet在英國史洛普爾厄普頓 可雷瑟特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1390年在他的家鄉享年35歲。

nameMeanings.name: Cressett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Derry): perhaps from Gaelic crosaid ‘little cross’. If so, it is likely to be a variant of Crossan . Compare the pair Corbett and Corban .

variants.name: Crossan
meanings: Irish (Donegal): shortened form of McCrossen , an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Chrosáin ‘son of the satirist’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Cressett was born in 1355, in Upton Cressett, Shropshire, England, his father, Thomas Cressett, was 26 and his mother, Constance de Upton, was 26. He married Florence Jane Corbet in 1385, in Upton Cressett, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1390, in his hometown, at the age of 35.
